Patch Set 3:

(8 comments)

My main comment would be to move to the in-kernel base64 implementation, except if we absolutely need the base64url encoding.

Also, I got my finger burnt debugging GSS with Lustre and I would appreciate a careful error management and error reporting. Try avoiding generic error code and message as much as possible :)

Patch Set 3:

Hi Neil, as you were maintainers of sunrpc code in kernel, I'm wondering what would be your take on Lustre GSS code hitting the buffer limit of sunrpc cache upcall mechanism (PAGE_SIZE) as nfsd did, long time ago. The move NFS did seem heavy weight and not worth doing.

Moving that to some alraedy existing Lustre mechanism like upcall_cache could be sufficient, except if you see a strong benefit to stick to cache_detail :/ ?

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Patch Set 3:

Should that one be abandoned?

Abandoned

This patch is replaced with #51946 on client side, and on server side with #52224 and #52305.

commit.subject: LU-17015 sec: support large Kerberos tokens
commit.message:
LU-17015 sec: support large Kerberos tokens

On client side, increase default size allocated for token in GSS
context init request, from 1024 to 4096. This will consume more memory
only for SEC_CTX_INIT requests, and in all cases buffers are shrunk
before being sent over the wire.
And when packing context init request, do not assert if token is
larger than pre-allocated size. Return an error instead.

On server side, change the format of sunrpc cache requests/responses
exchanged between kernel and user-space. The protocol only imposes
that each request or response record should be printable ASCII with
precisely one newline character which should be at the end.
So instead of printing data to hexadecimal format, which basically
doubles size used in buffer, just base64url encode it. It has a 4/3
ratio, which is much more efficient.
This format change does not require any compat flag, as this is
server-side internal processing.

Test-Parameters: kerberos=true testlist=sanity-krb5
Signed-off-by: Sebastien Buisson <sbuisson@ddn.com>
Change-Id: I7473a0b6d9d33a0e0627d4c7318f590237ae28ba
